sources.videoTitle: EARLY EXPERT TEAM! I *STOLE* this fantastic SHADOW TWISTER GYARADOS Team for th Jungle Cup
sources.author: JonkusPKMN

description: Double-ice Abomasnow lead with Shadow Twister Gyarados safe swap and Water Gun Quagsire closer. Team stolen from Exeutor on Twitter, designed as an early Expert/Veteran build for the Jungle Cup.
performanceNotes: Abomasnow's Icy Wind debuffs make cleanup easy for Quagsire and Gyarados
Shadow Gyarados with Twister applies strong fast pressure and baiting potential
Fortress is the hardest counter — if you don't face it, the team runs smoothly
Abomasnow shuts down the prevalent water types in Jungle Cup while providing valuable ice damage
Gyarados benefits from newly added Twister for fast energy cycling
Quagsire applies heavy pressure with rapid Aqua Tail spam and Stone Edge as a nuke option

sources.videoTitle: SHADOW ABOMASNOW LOCKS DOWN WATER-TYPES in the Jungle Cup!!
sources.author: XehrFelroseGO

description: Triple shadow team with Shadow Abomasnow as a core-breaking lead that shuts down water types with grass/ice coverage. Gyarados covers Abomasnow's fire and fighting weaknesses, while Shadow Quagsire closes with spammy Aqua Tails and Stone Edge nukes.

performanceNotes: Creator considers this the stronger of the two teams
Used by a trainer to reach 2600 ELO
Furret is a strong counter-swap into Quagsire safe switches; Camerupt's Rock Tomb + ground coverage hits basically everything for at least neutral
description: The stronger of the two teams per the creator. Shadow Camerupt lead with Furret as a strong counter-swap to Quagsire safe switches, and Altaria as closer. A trainer used this team to hit 2600 ELO.

sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: *DOUBLE BUFFED* Shadow Camerupt is DISGUSTING in the Jungle Cup! | Pokémon GO PvP

performanceNotes: Shadow Quagsire is a spammy menace with Mud Shot generating fast energy for Aqua Tail and Stone Edge
Corviknight's stored Payback can surprise opponents in the endgame
Performed well in the 2600s ELO range across consecutive sets

sources.videoTitle: Quagsire is a SPAMMY MENACE Once Again!! | Pokémon GO Battle League
sources.author: Reis2TheOccasion

description: Double flyer ABA structure with Corviknight lead, Shadow Quagsire safe swap, and Altaria closer. The two flyers function differently — Corviknight as a Steel type and Altaria as a Dragon — making the ABA less exploitable.

performanceNotes: Creator went approximately 23-2 with this team last season
Wins vs Piloswine lead and Altaria lead
Do 5 fast moves with Scizor before switching to Altaria vs Diggersby swap-ins to bank energy
description: Creator's personal team from last Jungle Cup season. Double shield Scizor in the lead. Altaria hard counters ground types like Diggersby that swap in. Forretress anchors the back.

sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Jungle Cup | Pokemon GO PvP
sources.author: YasserPoGO

sources.videoTitle: Togekiss Has The PERFECT Coverage For GBL [Pokémon GO PvP]
sources.author: ItsAXN

description: Water Gun Lanturn lead with Altaria safe swap and Togekiss closer in Jungle Cup. Togekiss provides Aura Sphere and Psyshock coverage which is strong in this format.
performanceNotes: Togekiss's Aura Sphere and Psyshock coverage is highlighted as strong in Jungle Cup
Water Gun Lanturn chosen over Spark variant for this team composition
Altaria serves as a pivot that can pressure Forretress and preserve Togekiss for endgame
Talonflame has two-shield win conditions against most of the top 10 meta picks
Criminally weak to dragons, Water Gun Lanturn, and Gyarados
Creator explicitly notes this is not recommended for climbing as the meta adapts

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: UNEXPECTED WATER DAMAGE | Water Gun QUAGSIRE Jungle Cup Team | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Shadow Talonflame lead in an ABB-style team designed as a hard meta read for Jungle Cup. Talonflame forces switches with double-shield pressure, while the back two (Altaria and Water Gun Quagsire) cover each other's weaknesses and rely on alignment. Team submission from Nerf Rampidos.

performanceNotes: Achieved a 13-2 run in Jungle Cup
Shadow Quagsire draws out threats that Camerupt doesn't want to face
Camerupt's dual 50-energy charge moves (Scorching Sands + Rock Tomb) provide excellent coverage

sources.videoTitle: INSANE 13-2 RUN with SHADOW CAMERUPT in the Jungle Cup!!
sources.author: XehrFelroseGO

description: Togekiss lead with Shadow Quagsire safe swap and Shadow Camerupt closer. Built on stream with chat, leveraging Camerupt's new Scorching Sands and Rock Tomb for broad neutral coverage.

performanceNotes: Water Gun Quagsire provides fast move pressure into flyers that threaten Whimsicott
Quagsire safe swap baits out opposing water/ground types so Camerupt can sweep uncontested
Shadow Camerupt with Rock Tomb debuffs and Scorching Sands closes games ruthlessly

sources.videoTitle: *DOUBLE BUFFED* Shadow Camerupt is DISGUSTING in the Jungle Cup! | Pokémon GO PvP
sources.author: HomeSliceHenry

description: Creator's own team. Whimsicott lead with Shadow Water Gun Quagsire as safe swap to pressure flyers and bait out opposing mud boys, leaving Shadow Camerupt free to sweep in the back.

performanceNotes: Creator went 15-9-1 climbing ELO in Jungle Cup
Originally suggested by Alpha Club Y2K community member who went 10-0
Non-shadow versions also work but shadows fit the glass cannon playstyle better

sources.author: XehrFelroseGO
sources.videoTitle: Doing MASSIVE DAMAGE & CLIMBING with this TRIPLE SHADOW TEAM in the Jungle Cup!!

description: Triple shadow team where Shadow Drapion leads to pressure shields, Shadow Malamar cleans up in the mid-game, and Shadow Charizard closes as the sweeper with Ember, leveraging shield and energy advantages to roast entire teams.

performanceNotes: Reached season-high 2375 ELO after one set
Charjabug holds a special place for Dan — helped him reach Toronto Regional Finals
Main weakness is Clodsire; otherwise Charjabug chips well even in bad matchups

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: SWAMPERT META AGAIN  | Jungle Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Tryhard team Dan switched to after the Corviknight team. Charjabug is bulky with good coverage via X-Scissor, Swampert provides Hydro Cannon pressure, and Shadow Piloswine closes.

performanceNotes: Creator recommends Shadow Diggersby over regular
Flygons remain a problem but Piloswine handles them
Cloyster closes games after Diggersby baits out counters

sources.author: YasserPoGO
sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Jungle Cup | Pokemon GO PvP

description: Top-3 PvPoke team. Lead Piloswine stays in vs Altaria/Flygon. Shadow Diggersby safe swap baits out Cloyster's counters so Cloyster can sweep in the back.

performanceNotes: Water Gun Quagsire chosen over Mud Shot for superior water-type damage in Jungle Cup meta
Shadow Gyarados resists non-Volt Switch Forretress fast moves and outpaces it
Corviknight plus Quagsire form a strong switch core backing up the Gyarados swap
description: Shadow Water Gun Quagsire lead with Shadow Dragon Breath Gyarados as the safe swap and Corviknight as the closer. Gyarados leverages the buffed Twister as a pseudo-dragon attacker while Aqua Tail provides fast pressure.

sources.author: ItsAXN
sources.videoTitle: BUFFED Twister Gyarados Is A MENACE In Jungle Cup [Pokémon GO PvP]

description: Classic core of Swampert + Corviknight with Umbreon as the OG safe swap. Corviknight and Umbreon provide enough bulk to save shields for Swampert's Hydro Cannon spam.

sources.videoTitle: SWAMPERT META AGAIN  | Jungle Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League
sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

performanceNotes: Climbed from 2200 to 2350 ELO in Jungle Cup
Umbreon's insane bulk allows it to survive even at low HP, enabling foul play spam
Swampert's Hydro Cannon takes out most Pokemon in two hits doing over 50% each
Reached Rank 6 in the world and Expert rank on the first GBL leaderboards of the season
Dunsparce added specifically to handle the rise of Stunfisk leads that destroyed the previous Togekiss lead
Qwilfish with Scald catches opponents off guard and can solo entire teams when ahead on energy
description: Dunsparce lead with Qwilfish safe swap running the unusual Scald set instead of Ice Beam, and Gourgeist as closer. The team revolves around building energy on Qwilfish and leveraging Scald's cheap cost and high neutral damage to sweep.

sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: I hit *Rank 6 in the World* with a Pokémon moveset that no one expected! | Pokémon GO Battle League

performanceNotes: Emolga ranked 101 overall but has a 21-23 record vs top meta with 484 average, performing like a top 30-40 Pokémon
Dominates the flyer-heavy meta (Talonflame, Gyarados, Togekiss, Corviknight, Pelipper)
Weak to mud boys (Quagsire, Swampert) and other electric leads

sources.videoTitle: I BROKE THE META TOO LATE | EMOLGA is the HIDDEN ANSWER | Pokémon GO Battle League
sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

description: Emolga lead exploiting the flyer-heavy Jungle Cup meta, with Furret and Weezing as spammy safe swaps that apply shield pressure so Emolga can land Acrobatics. Brick Break on Furret chosen over Trailblaze to hit Corviknight and Forretress.

performanceNotes: Achieved a 13-game win streak, reaching close to 2600 ELO
Brick Break on Furret is key to beating Lickilicky and dropping its defense
Whimsicott double-resists Gyarados's Aqua Tail + Twister making it the hardest counter in the meta

sources.videoTitle: FULL ANTI META TEAM! I won 13 games in a ROW with WHIMSICOTT in the Jungle Cup
sources.author: JonkusPKMN

description: Full anti-meta Jungle Cup team built around Whimsicott to hard-counter Quagsire and Gyarados. Fletchinder leads with Peck instead of a fire fast move, Furret runs Brick Break to punish Lickilicky, and Whimsicott closes by double-resisting Gyarados's Aqua Tail and Twister.

sources.videoTitle: Gyarados puts on INSANE PRESSURE with BUFFED WATERFALL in the Jungle Cup!!
sources.author: XehrFelroseGO

description: Waterfall Gyarados lead with double shadow ground backline. Gyarados applies fast move pressure with buffed Waterfall while Shadow Gliscor and Shadow Flygon provide Sand Tomb debuff spam and ground-type coverage in the back.
performanceNotes: Waterfall Gyarados shreds Piloswine before it reaches a second charge move
Double ground backline with Sand Tomb spam forces shields and drops defense
Team used by Rebel 8bit climbing to Ace rank

description: Forretress lead with Furret as safe swap and Togekiss as closer. Forretress covers Togekiss's poison weakness while Togekiss sweeps with Aura Sphere late game.

sources.videoTitle: TOGEKISS is a TOP TIER Jungle Cup Pick | Jungle Cup Team | Pokémon GO
sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

performanceNotes: Forretress only weak to fire, great into ice leads common in Jungle Cup
Togekiss with energy is devastating — Aura Sphere pressures steels and poisons that counter it
Creator at 2300+ ELO, described the games as pretty easy overall
Went 13-2 on the day
Scrafty is a near must-play with all the normal/ice/rock/steel in the top 20
Lickilicky provides flexibility against Talonflame leads without needing to keep Gyarados in

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: GYARADOS goes WILD  | Jungle Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Shadow Gyarados lead with Lickilicky safe swap and Scrafty closer. Remix of a previous Jungle Cup team, replacing Cradily with Lickilicky for better Talonflame flexibility.

performanceNotes: Climbed from 2200 to 2350 ELO in a day using this team
Swalot and Toucannon cover each other's weaknesses and apply heavy shield pressure with spammy charged moves
Non-shadow Toucannon preferred for bulk to absorb hits in the back

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: SWALOT a DOMINANT SLEEPER PICK | Jungle Cup Team | Pokémon GO

description: Toucannon lead to grab shields or farm energy, Lickilicky safe swap to absorb hits and spam Body Slams, then sweep with the Swalot + Toucannon backline combo.

A debuff-heavy team that overwhelms opponents with fast move pressure combined with attack and defense drops from Icy Wind, Psychic Fangs, and Poison Fang. The creator copied this team from an opponent faced in a previous video.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The MOST TOXIC TEAM wins you battles in SECONDS in the Jungle Cup!

performanceNotes: Creator went on a 5-0 win streak to start
Battles end extremely fast due to heavy fast move pressure and double super effective damage scenarios
Debuffing opponent defense makes already strong fast moves (Charm, shadow-boosted Sucker Punch, Thunder Fang) even more devastating
Scrafty dominates common leads like Diggersby, Piloswine, Lickilicky, and Stunfisk
Talonflame covers Scrafty's fighting and fairy weaknesses as a fire/flying type
Quagsire identified as the team's main weakness due to super effective mud shot and water moves
description: Scrafty lead exploiting the many fighting-weak Pokémon in Jungle Cup, with Weezing as a safe swap and Talonflame as the closer. Simple, easy-to-build balanced team.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: So MANY THINGS WEAK TO FIGHTING | Scrafty THRIVES in Jungle Cup | Pokémon GO
